questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in $10$ hours while pipe B empties it in $15$ hours. With both open, the tank fills in
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Subtract the emptying rate from the filling rate.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{10}-\dfrac{1}{15}=\dfrac{3-2}{30}=\dfrac{1}{30}$, so $30$ hours.

**Answer.** $30$ hours.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In what ratio should rice costing $20$ per kg be mixed with rice costing $30$ per kg so that the mixture costs $24$ per kg?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Alligation: ratio $=$ (dearer $-$ mean) : (mean $-$ cheaper).

**Working.** $(30-24):(24-20)=6:4=3:2$ (cheaper : dearer).

**Answer.** $3:2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In an examination $40\%$ marks are required to pass. A student who scores $178$ marks fails by $22$ marks. The maximum marks of the examination are _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pass mark = score + shortfall.

**Working.** Pass mark $=178+22=200=0.40\,M$, so
$$M=\frac{200}{0.40}=500$$

**Answer.** $500$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $5000$ is divided among A, B and C in the ratio $2:3:5$. C's share is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share = total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** C's fraction $=\dfrac{5}{2+3+5}=\dfrac{5}{10}$, so $5000\times\dfrac{1}{2}=2500$.

**Answer.** $2500$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows annual sales (in units) for four years: $40, 60, 30, 80$. The highest annual sales figure is _____ units.

figure.caption: Bar chart of annual sales for 2018-2021: 40, 60, 30, 80 units.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='40' y='100' width='28' height='60' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='80' y='70' width='28' height='90' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='120' y='115' width='28' height='45' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='160' y='40' width='28' height='120' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='44' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2018</text><text x='84' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2019</text><text x='124' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2020</text><text x='164' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2021</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Read the tallest bar.

**Working.** The values are $40,60,30,80$; the maximum is $80$ (year 2021).

**Answer.** $80$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many straight line segments are needed to form the capital letter shown?

figure.caption: The capital letter E drawn with straight strokes.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='20' x2='30' y2='120' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='4'/><line x1='30' y1='20' x2='75' y2='20' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='4'/><line x1='30' y1='70' x2='65' y2='70' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='4'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='75' y2='120' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='4'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count each distinct stroke.

**Working.** The letter E has one vertical stroke and three horizontal strokes: $1+3=4$.

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio $1:2:3$. The largest angle is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angles of a triangle sum to $180^\circ$.

**Working.** Parts $=1+2+3=6$; largest $=\dfrac{3}{6}\times 180 = 90^\circ$.

**Answer.** $90$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is B's brother. C is A's mother. D is C's father. How is B related to D?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Build the family tree level by level.

**Working.** C is the mother of A and B; D is C's father, hence the grandparent of A and B. So B is D's grandchild.

**Answer.** Grandchild.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An arrow points to the north-east. In a vertical mirror, the arrow appears to point toward the

figure.caption: An arrow pointing to the north-east beside a dashed vertical mirror line.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='95' x2='95' y2='35' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='3'/><polygon points='95,35 80,38 90,50' fill='#6366f1'/><line x1='140' y1='12' x2='140' y2='108' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A vertical mirror reverses the east-west component while keeping north-south.

**Working.** North-east (up and to the right) becomes up and to the left, i.e. north-west.

**Answer.** North-West.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same $3\times3\times3$ painted cube cut into $27$ small cubes, the number with exactly one painted face is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube with grid lines on its visible faces.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-face cubes sit at the centre of each face.

**Working.** A cube has $6$ faces, each with one centre cube: $6\times1=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock element is loaded to principal stresses $\sigma_1=60\ \text{MPa}$ and $\sigma_3=20\ \text{MPa}$ (figure). Compute the maximum shear stress (MPa).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau_{max}=\dfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$.

**Working.**
$$\tau_{max}=\frac{60-20}{2}=20\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $20$ MPa.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The value of $\displaystyle\oint_{C}(2x\,dx+2y\,dy)$ around any closed curve $C$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The integrand is the exact differential $d(x^{2}+y^{2})$, so the field $(2x,2y)=\nabla(x^{2}+y^{2})$ is conservative.

**Working.** The line integral of a gradient around any closed loop is $0$.

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Coward's explosibility (triangle) diagram is used to
options: show whether a mixture of methane, air and inert gas is explosive, non-explosive or potentially explosive on dilution
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Explosibility depends on fuel and oxygen together.

**Reasoning.** Coward's diagram plots CH₄ vs effective oxygen and bounds the explosive zone; a sample's point shows if it is safe, explosive, or would become explosive if mixed with air — vital when sealing/reopening fire areas.

**Answer.** Classify CH₄/air/inert mixtures by explosibility.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: A simple circular curve of radius $200$ m has a deflection angle $\Delta = 60^\circ$. Compute the tangent length (m).

figure.caption: Simple circular curve
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><path d='M30 150 A150 150 0 0 1 170 60' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='200' y2='150' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='200' y1='150' x2='170' y2='60' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='30' cy='150' r='3' fill='#1f2937'/><circle cx='170' cy='60' r='3' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='35' y='170' font-size='8'>R=200 m, deflection=60 deg</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tangent length $T=R\tan(\Delta/2)$.

**Working.**
$$T=200\tan 30^\circ=200\times0.5774=115.47\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $115.47$ m.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A point source of luminous intensity $200\ \text{cd}$ illuminates a surface $4\ \text{m}$ away, normal to the beam (figure). Compute the illuminance (lux).

figure.caption: Inverse-square illumination
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='40' cy='60' r='12' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#f59e0b'><line x1='52' y1='60' x2='180' y2='30'/><line x1='52' y1='60' x2='180' y2='90'/></g><text x='32' y='120' font-size='8'>I=200 cd, distance 4 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $E=\dfrac{I}{d^2}$.

**Working.**
$$E=\frac{200}{4^2}=\frac{200}{16}=12.5\ \text{lux}$$

**Answer.** $12.5$ lux.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the Mohr circle shown ($\sigma_1=80$, $\sigma_3=20\ \mathrm{MPa}$), the maximum shear stress (radius), in MPa, is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau_{max}=\dfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$.

**Working.**
$$\tau_{max}=\frac{80-20}{2}=30\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $30$ MPa.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Fourier series of an ODD periodic function (such as the square wave shown) contains

figure.caption: An odd square wave: f(-x) = -f(x).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='10' y1='80' x2='250' y2='80' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='130' y1='20' x2='130' y2='140' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='10,120 70,120 70,40 130,40 130,120 190,120 190,40 250,40' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><text x='200' y='34' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>+A</text><text x='40' y='134' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>-A</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** An odd function $f(-x)=-f(x)$ has all cosine (even) Fourier coefficients zero, including the constant $a_0$.

**Working.** Only the sine terms $b_n\sin(n\omega x)$ survive.

**Answer.** Only sine terms.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: To provide an average of 50 lux over a 100 m² work area, the total luminous flux required (ignoring losses), in lumens, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Flux $=$ illuminance $\times$ area.

**Working.**
$$\Phi=50\times100=5000\ \text{lm}$$

**Answer.** 5000 lumens.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A body starts from rest and accelerates at $4\,\text{m/s}^2$ over $8\,\text{m}$. Its final velocity is _____ m/s.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The 'effective grade' (total resistance expressed as an equivalent grade) experienced by a haul truck is
options: always equal to the rolling resistance alone
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rolling resistance can be expressed as an equivalent grade.

**Reasoning.** The truck's engine sees the sum of the actual climb and the rolling drag; adding the two percentages gives one effective grade against which rimpull/speed are read from performance charts.

**Answer.** Grade % + rolling-resistance %.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: From the Venn diagram, $P(A)=0.5$, $P(B)=0.4$ and $P(A\cap B)=0.2$. Compute $P(A\cup B)$.

figure.caption: Venn diagram of two events
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='70' r='45' fill='#bfdbfe' fill-opacity='0.6' stroke='#2563eb'/><circle cx='135' cy='70' r='45' fill='#fca5a5' fill-opacity='0.6' stroke='#dc2626'/><text x='52' y='75' font-size='9'>0.5</text><text x='150' y='75' font-size='9'>0.4</text><text x='100' y='75' font-size='9'>0.2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P(A\cup B)=P(A)+P(B)-P(A\cap B)$.

**Working.**
$$P(A\cup B)=0.5+0.4-0.2=0.7$$

**Answer.** $0.7$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which properties of definite integrals are ALWAYS valid?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Basic integral properties.

**Working.**
- Reversing limits flips sign — TRUE.
- Zero-width interval gives $0$ — TRUE.
- Linearity (sum rule) — TRUE.
- Integral of a product is NOT the product of integrals — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (c).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The margin of safety is
options: the excess of actual (or budgeted) sales over break-even sales
the fixed cost divided by price
questions.solution:
**Concept.** It measures how far sales can fall before a loss.

**Working.** Margin of safety $=$ actual sales $-$ break-even sales.

**Answer.** the excess of actual sales over break-even sales.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A winding rope is 650 m long with a linear mass of 4 kg/m. Its total mass, in kg, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mass $=$ length $\times$ linear mass.

**Working.**
$$m=650\times4=2600\ \text{kg}$$

**Answer.** 2600 kg.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Using $\sigma_p=\dfrac{\sigma_v}{1-e}$ with $\sigma_v=10\,\text{MPa}$ and extraction ratio $e=0.75$, the pillar stress is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_p=\dfrac{\sigma_v}{1-e}$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_p=\frac{10}{1-0.75}=\frac{10}{0.25}=40.$$

**Answer.** $40\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A blasthole is advanced $12\ \text{m}$ in $4\ \text{min}$ (figure). Compute the average penetration rate (m/min).

figure.caption: Drilling penetration
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='60' y='20' width='16' height='40' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='64' y='60' width='8' height='80' fill='#6b7280'/><rect x='30' y='140' width='100' height='20' fill='#a8a29e' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='60' x2='100' y2='140' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#dr)'/><defs><marker id='dr' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L3 6 L6 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='14' y='130' font-size='8'>12 m in 4 min</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Penetration rate $=\dfrac{\text{depth}}{\text{time}}$.

**Working.**
$$r=\frac{12}{4}=3\ \text{m/min}$$

**Answer.** $3$ m/min.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^{n}=$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Geometric series $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}ar^{n-1}=\dfrac{a}{1-r}$ for $|r|<1$; here first term $a=\tfrac12$, ratio $r=\tfrac12$.

**Working.**
$$S=\frac{1/2}{1-1/2}=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: In Barton's Q-system, the ratio $J_r/J_a$ broadly represents
options: the shear strength (roughness vs alteration) of the joints
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $J_r$ (roughness) over $J_a$ (alteration) reflects inter-block frictional strength.

**Working.** Rougher, unaltered joints give higher $J_r/J_a$ and more shear strength.

**Answer.** the shear strength of the joints.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: When computing RQD, the core pieces that are summed are those that are
options: intact and at least 100 mm long
questions.solution:
**Concept.** RQD counts only sound pieces $\ge 10\,\text{cm}$.

**Working.** Only intact lengths of at least 100 mm contribute.

**Answer.** intact and at least 100 mm long.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Compared with a long rib pillar, the tributary-area stress formula for a square pillar uses
options: the square of the width ratio instead of the linear ratio
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Square pillars draw load from two horizontal directions.

**Working.** Hence $\left(\tfrac{W_p+W_o}{W_p}\right)^2$ versus the linear ratio for rib pillars.

**Answer.** the square of the width ratio instead of the linear ratio.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: If the longwall shown advances by one 0.8 m web per shear and takes 10 shears per day, compute the daily face advance (m/day).

figure.caption: Longwall advance
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='70' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='14' height='70' fill='#94a3b8'/><line x1='44' y1='115' x2='62' y2='115' stroke='#475569' marker-end='url(#aa)'/><defs><marker id='aa' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='70' y='119' font-size='8'>web 0.8 m, 10 shears/day</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Daily advance = web × number of shears.

**Working.**
$$A = 0.8 \times 10 = 8$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{8}$ m/day.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The distribution sketched has a long tail to the right. It is

figure.caption: A distribution with a long right tail.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='125' x2='225' y2='125' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M30 125 C50 120 60 40 85 40 C110 40 130 110 175 120 C200 124 210 125 215 125' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.18)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><text x='150' y='100' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>long right tail</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Skew direction follows the longer tail.

**Working.** A tail extending to the right indicates positive (right) skew, with mean $>$ median $>$ mode.

**Answer.** Positively (right) skewed.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Detonating cord (e.g. Cordtex) is
options: a slow-burning fuse used to fire a plain detonator
a flexible cord with a high-VOD PETN core used to initiate charges along the trunkline/down the hole
an inert tube carrying flushing air
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Detonating cord detonates (it does not burn slowly).

**Reasoning.** Its PETN core detonates at ~6500–7000 m/s, so it reliably initiates boosters/charges and links holes on the surface trunkline; it is fired by a detonator.

**Answer.** High-VOD PETN-cored initiating cord.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A truck's full cycle time (load + haul + dump + return + spot) is 20 min and its loading time at the shovel is 4 min. The number of trucks needed to keep one shovel continuously busy is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Match factor: trucks per shovel $=\dfrac{\text{truck cycle time}}{\text{loading time}}$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{20}{4}=5$$

**Answer.** 5 trucks.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Doubling Time
questions.stem: Using the rule of $72$, the approximate time for money to double at $10\%$ per year is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rule of 72: $t\approx 72/i\%$.

**Working.**
$$t=\frac{72}{10}=7.2.$$

**Answer.** $7.2$ years.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Alternative A yields $\textsf{Rs}\,100$ with probability $0.6$ and $-\textsf{Rs}\,20$ with probability $0.4$. Its expected monetary value is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a Poisson distribution with mean $\lambda=3$, the probability $P(X=0)$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Poisson probability mass function with mean lambda = 3 (right-skewed, peak near k = 2, 3).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='240' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='38' y='136' width='20' height='24' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='66' y='87' width='20' height='73' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='94' y='50' width='20' height='110' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='122' y='50' width='20' height='110' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='150' y='77' width='20' height='83' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='178' y='110' width='20' height='50' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='206' y='135' width='20' height='25' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='44' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>0</text><text x='100' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2</text><text x='212' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>6</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P(X=k)=\dfrac{e^{-\lambda}\lambda^{k}}{k!}$.

**Working.**
$$P(X=0)=e^{-3}=0.0498\approx 0.05$$

**Answer.** $\approx 0.05$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A kata thermometer is used in mine-climate work to measure
options: the cooling power of the air (and hence low air velocities/comfort)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The kata measures heat-loss (cooling) rate.

**Reasoning.** The time for the heated kata bulb to cool through a fixed range gives the air's cooling power, from which low air velocities and the comfort of the environment can be assessed.

**Answer.** Cooling power of the air.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A circular duct of diameter $0.6\,\text{m}$ carries air at $10\,\text{m/s}$. The quantity delivered is _____ m$^3$/s. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=A v=\dfrac{\pi}{4}d^2 v$.

**Working.**
$$A=\frac{\pi}{4}(0.6)^2=0.2827,\quad Q=0.2827\times10=2.83.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 2.83\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Using Obert-Duvall Sp = S1(0.778 + 0.222 w/h) with S1 = 8 MPa, w = 15 m and h = 3 m, the pillar strength, in MPa, is ____ (one decimal).

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A conveyor lifts 2880 t/h of material through a vertical height of 20 m. Ignoring friction and taking g = 9.81 m/s², the lifting power, in kW, is ____ (nearest whole number).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Lift power $=\dot m\,g\,h$, with mass flow in kg/s.

**Working.**
$$\dot m=\frac{2880\times1000}{3600}=800\ \text{kg/s}$$
$$P=800\times9.81\times20=156960\ \text{W}\approx157\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** 157 kW.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The vertical in-situ stress in rock is most commonly estimated from
options: the weight of the overburden per unit area
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gravitational loading governs vertical stress.

**Working.** $\sigma_v=\gamma H$, the overburden weight per unit area.

**Answer.** the weight of the overburden per unit area.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Glare in mine lighting is
options: excessive or poorly shielded brightness in the field of view causing visual discomfort and reduced visibility
the same as illuminance in lux
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Glare is uncontrolled brightness contrast.

**Reasoning.** Bright unshielded lamps in the line of sight dazzle the eye and lower the ability to see detail; it is controlled by shielding/diffusing luminaires and positioning them out of direct view.

**Answer.** Discomforting excessive brightness.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A parallel combination of equivalent resistance $1\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ has a pressure drop of $400\,\text{Pa}$. The total quantity is _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=\sqrt{P/R_{eq}}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=\sqrt{\frac{400}{1}}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The solution of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}=2x$ with $y(1)=2$ gives $y(3)=$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Integrate and apply the initial condition.

**Working.**
$$y=x^{2}+C,\quad y(1)=2\Rightarrow C=1,\quad y(3)=9+1=10$$

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The area moment of inertia of a circle of diameter $4$ about a centroidal diameter is _____ (units$^4$). Round to two decimals.

figure.caption: A circle of diameter 4 with a centroidal diametral axis.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='70' cy='60' r='45' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.10)' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='60' x2='125' y2='60' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=0$ everywhere, the net outward flux of $\mathbf{F}$ through ANY closed surface is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** By the divergence theorem, flux $=\iiint_V(\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F})\,dV$.

**Working.** A divergence-free field gives $\iiint_V 0\,dV=0$ for every closed surface.

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A drill jumbo is used for
options: large-diameter rotary blasthole drilling in opencast benches
multi-boom hydraulic percussive drilling of the face in tunnels/drives
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Jumbos drill development rounds underground.

**Reasoning.** A jumbo carries two or more hydraulic drifters on booms to drill the many parallel holes of a tunnel/drift face quickly and accurately for blasting.

**Answer.** Multi-boom face drilling in tunnels/drives.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a normal distribution with $\mu=50$ and $\sigma=10$, the $z$-score of the value $x=70$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $z=\dfrac{x-\mu}{\sigma}$.

**Working.**
$$z=\frac{70-50}{10}=2$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The average value of $f(x)=x^{2}$ over the interval $[0,3]$ is _____ .

figure.caption: y = x^2 on [0, 3]; the horizontal line marks the average value (= 3) over the interval.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='40' x2='30' y2='175' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='170' x2='215' y2='170' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='30,170 90,157 150,116 210,49' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='210' y2='130' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='5 3'/><text x='150' y='124' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>average = 3</text><text x='198' y='184' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>x = 3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average value $=\dfrac{1}{b-a}\int_a^b f\,dx$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{3-0}\int_{0}^{3}x^{2}\,dx=\frac{1}{3}\cdot\frac{27}{3}=\frac{1}{3}\cdot 9=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: For a given true thickness, the width of outcrop of an inclined bed on flat ground is greatest when the bed
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gentle dips spread the outcrop.

**Reasoning.** On level ground the outcrop width $=t/\sin\delta$ (t = true thickness); as the dip δ decreases, $\sin\delta$ falls and the width increases, becoming a minimum for a vertical bed.

**Answer.** Gentle dip.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Doubling Time
questions.stem: The exact number of years for money to double at $10\%$ compounded annually is _____ . Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Which of the following are environmental impacts associated with mining? (Select all that apply.)
options: Acid mine drainage and surface-water pollution
A guaranteed improvement in regional groundwater quality
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mining disturbs land, water and air.

**Reasoning.** Excavation and subsidence degrade land, sulphide oxidation acidifies water (AMD), and operations raise dust — all adverse impacts. Mining does not inherently improve groundwater quality; if anything it can degrade it.

**Answer.** Options 1, 2 and 3.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: An ore deposit cost $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}00{,}000$ and contains $5{,}00{,}000$ tonnes of recoverable ore. The depletion charge per tonne is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Depletion rate $=\dfrac{\text{cost}}{\text{total reserve}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{10000000}{500000}=20.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,20$ per tonne.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The area enclosed between the parabola $y=4-x^{2}$ and the $x$-axis (shaded) is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Region bounded by y = 4 - x^2 and the x-axis, from x = -2 to x = 2.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='140' x2='220' y2='140' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='120' y1='20' x2='120' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M40 140 Q120 -12 200 140 Z' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.18)' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><text x='150' y='60' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>y = 4 - x^2</text><text x='34' y='154' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>-2</text><text x='196' y='154' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The curve crosses the axis at $x=\pm2$; area $=\int_{-2}^{2}(4-x^{2})\,dx$.

**Working.**
$$\int_{-2}^{2}(4-x^{2})\,dx=\Big[4x-\tfrac{x^{3}}{3}\Big]_{-2}^{2}=2\left(8-\tfrac{8}{3}\right)=\tfrac{32}{3}\approx 10.67$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac{32}{3}\approx 10.67$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Compared with surface surveying, underground mine surveying is more difficult chiefly because of
options: the absence of any need for accuracy
cramped workings, artificial lighting, short and steeply inclined sights, and stations fixed in the roof
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The working environment constrains observation.

**Reasoning.** Restricted space, poor light, dust, short/inclined lines of sight and roof-mounted stations all complicate underground work; orientation must also be carried down a shaft.

**Answer.** Confined, poorly lit conditions with roof stations.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway has a pressure loss of $60\,\text{Pa}$. If only the friction factor $k$ is doubled (flow unchanged), the new pressure loss is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $R\propto k$ and $P=RQ^2$, so $P\propto k$.

**Working.**
$$P_2=2\times60=120.$$

**Answer.** $120\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A systematic error such as using a tape that is uniformly too long is characterised by being
options: of constant sign, cumulative, and removable by calibration/correction
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Systematic errors follow a definite law.

**Reasoning.** They have the same sign each time and accumulate with the number of measurements; once the cause is known (e.g. tape length) a correction removes them.

**Answer.** Constant sign, cumulative, correctable.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Evaluate $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{1} x\,e^{x}\,dx$ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Integration by parts $\int u\,dv=uv-\int v\,du$ with $u=x$, $dv=e^{x}dx$.

**Working.**
$$\int_{0}^{1}x e^{x}dx=\big[x e^{x}-e^{x}\big]_{0}^{1}=(e-e)-(0-1)=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: An air stream of mass flow 50 kg/s is to be cooled by 5 °C. Taking $c_p=1.005$ kJ/kg·K, the refrigeration (cooling) duty, in kW, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\dot{Q}=\dot{m}\,c_p\,\Delta T$.

**Working.**
$$\dot{Q}=50\times1.005\times5=251.25\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** 251.25 kW.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A sunk cost is
options: a past cost that is irrelevant to current decisions
a future cost that varies with output
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sunk costs are already incurred and unrecoverable.

**Working.** They should be excluded from forward-looking decisions.

**Answer.** a past cost that is irrelevant to current decisions.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The solution of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}=x$ with $y(0)=1$ gives $y(2)=$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Integrate directly and apply the initial condition.

**Working.**
$$y=\tfrac{x^{2}}{2}+C,\quad y(0)=1\Rightarrow C=1,\quad y(2)=\tfrac{4}{2}+1=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: A 'stratabound' ore deposit is one that is
options: confined to a particular stratigraphic unit or bed
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stratabound ores honour stratigraphy.

**Reasoning.** The mineralisation is restricted to a specific bed/horizon (though it may be disseminated within it); strata-form deposits additionally lie parallel to bedding.

**Answer.** Restricted to a particular stratum.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A drainage pump lifts $0.08\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ of water through a total head of $120\ \text{m}$ at an overall efficiency of $0.75$. Compute the input power (kW, two decimals). Take $\rho g=9810\ \mathrm{N/m^3}$.

figure.caption: Mine drainage pump
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='60' cy='100' r='22' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='60' y1='78' x2='60' y2='30' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3' marker-end='url(#pp)'/><defs><marker id='pp' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L3 6 L6 0 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='12' y='135' font-size='8'>Q=0.08 m^3/s, H=120 m, eff=0.75</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Input power $=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$.

**Working.**
$$P=\frac{9810\times0.08\times120}{0.75}=\frac{94176}{0.75}=125568\ \text{W}=125.57\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** $125.57$ kW.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Two components of reliability $0.9$ and $0.8$ operate in series. The system reliability is _____ .
